companyNameJapanese: ステラケミファ株式会社
description: World leader in ultra-high-purity hydrofluoric acid (12-nine / 99.9999999999% purity). Founded 1916 in Osaka. Two segments: High-Purity Chemicals (86% of sales) and Transportation. Semiconductor chemicals (etching/cleaning) ~70% of revenue, energy/battery materials ~15%, optical/general ~15%. Only two companies globally (Stella and Morita Chemical) can produce 12-nine purity HF. Also manufactures LiPF6 (lithium hexafluorophosphate) for Li-ion battery electrolytes, fluoride compounds for optical lenses (stepper lenses, camera lenses), enriched boron-10 for nuclear applications, BF3 gas (Japan's sole manufacturer), and GMP-grade fluorides for pharmaceuticals. Revenue FY2025/3: 36.29B JPY (+19.2% YoY). Operating profit: 4.34B JPY (+59.4%). Employees: 289 (standalone). Factories: Sakai (Osaka), Izumi-Otsu (Osaka), Kitakyushu (Fukuoka). Subsidiaries in Singapore (Jurong Island manufacturing), China (Zhejiang, Quzhou, Shanghai). Exports to Korea, Taiwan, Malaysia, USA. Global EG-HF market ~$1.39B (2025), projected $1.87B by 2030.

majorClients: TSMC (CMP-0017) - ultra-high-purity HF for etching/cleaning, largest global foundry
Samsung Electronics (CMP-0018) - HF for memory (DRAM/NAND) and foundry fabs in Korea
Intel (CMP-0019) - HF for fab operations in USA/Ireland/Israel
SK Hynix (CMP-0020) - HF for DRAM/HBM fabs in Korea and China (Dalian/Wuxi)
Micron Technology (CMP-0021) - HF for DRAM/NAND fabs in Japan (Hiroshima), USA, Singapore
Kioxia (CMP-0014) - HF for NAND flash fabs in Yokkaichi and Kitakami, Japan
SMIC (CMP-0066) - HF for China's #1 foundry, supplied via Shanghai/Zhejiang subsidiaries
Hua Hong Semiconductor (CMP-0070) - HF for China's #2 foundry in Shanghai/Wuxi
UMC (CMP-0102) - HF for Taiwan's #2 foundry, fabs in Taiwan/Singapore/China

Maruzen Chemicals Co., Ltd. (private, not in DB) - named as one of two largest revenue customers; 130-year-old Osaka chemical trading company, acts as distributor/reseller of Stella products domestically and globally (offices in USA, Germany, China, Taiwan, Singapore, Vietnam)

Mitsubishi Chemical Corporation (not in DB) - named as one of two largest revenue customers; buys LiPF6 lithium salt to formulate Li-ion battery electrolytes (Sol-Rite brand), which end up in Panasonic battery cells for Tesla/EV applications
LCD/display panel manufacturers (not individually identified) - HF for surface treatment of LCD display glass, BOE/JDI/Sharp are likely customers given Stella's product focus on LCD glass treatment
Japanese domestic semiconductor fabs (not individually identified) - Stella supplies 'almost all domestic semiconductor manufacturers' per Japanese industry sources
Rapidus / JASM / other new Japan fabs (potential) - new Japanese fab buildout creates additional demand
majorSuppliers: Fluorite/fluorspar (CaF2) mining companies - primary raw material for HF production. Stella has diversified procurement beyond China to include Europe and Mexico. China produces 60%+ of world fluorite. Key global producers: Orbia/Mexichem (Mexico, world's largest mine in San Luis Potosi), MINERSA Group (Spain/South Africa), Chinese miners. Specific supplier names not publicly disclosed.
Sulfuric acid suppliers (not disclosed) - H2SO4 reacts with CaF2 to produce HF; industrial-grade sulfuric acid is commodity input
Lithium compounds suppliers (not disclosed) - raw materials for LiPF6 production at Quzhou BDX subsidiary in China
Zhejiang Blue Star Chemical Co., Ltd. (subsidiary, 55% owned) - JV in Jinhua, Zhejiang; manufactures hydrofluoric acid, likely sources local Chinese fluorite
Quzhou BDX New Chemical Materials Co., Ltd. (subsidiary) - manufactures LiPF6 in Quzhou, Zhejiang, China
competitors: Morita Chemical Industries Co., Ltd. (private, Japan) - the ONLY other company capable of 12-nine purity HF. Founded 1917 in Osaka. 100+ year fluorine chemistry heritage. Chinese subsidiary Zhejiang Morita has 50,000 tons/year EG-HF capacity. Together with Stella, they supply 90%+ of Japanese domestic demand and dominate global ultra-high-purity segment.

Daikin Industries, Ltd. (6367.T, Japan) - major fluorine chemicals producer, leverages vertical integration and fluorspar resources for high-purity HF and fluorinated etching gases. Also competes in fluoropolymer resins.
Honeywell International Inc. (HON, USA) - electronic-grade HF producer, identified as a 'star player' in EG-HF market by MarketsandMarkets
Solvay SA (SOLB.BR, Belgium) - HF and fluorine specialty chemicals, subsidiary Zhejiang Lansol produces in China. Identified as market leader alongside Honeywell.

Do-Fluoride New Materials Co., Ltd. (002407.SZ, China) - Chinese EG-HF producer, quality has reached UP-SSS level (G5), the highest grade for semiconductor use. Also produces LiPF6 (direct competitor in battery segment). Major capacity expansion in recent years.
Kanto Denka Kogyo Co., Ltd. (CMP-0045, 4047.T, Japan) - fluorine specialty gas manufacturer, competes in fluorine-based semiconductor chemicals. Also produces LiPF6 for batteries.
Jiangyin Jianghua Microelectronics Materials Co., Ltd. (China) - Chinese EG-HF producer identified in market reports
Zhejiang Kaiheng Electronic Materials (China) - Chinese electronic-grade HF producer
Shaowu Fluoride (China) - Chinese HF producer, identified as leading player
Yingpeng Group (China) - Chinese fluorine chemical group
FDAC / Formosa Daikin Advanced Chemicals (Taiwan) - JV producing EG-HF
FUJIFILM Wako Pure Chemical Corporation (Japan) - ultra-pure chemical supplier including HF
NACALAI TESQUE, INC. (Japan) - chemical reagent supplier
subsidiaries: Stella Chemifa Singapore Pte Ltd. (100%, Jurong Island) - manufactures and sells high-purity chemicals, serves Southeast Asian semiconductor fabs
Zhejiang Blue Star Chemical Co., Ltd. (55% JV, Jinhua, Zhejiang) - manufactures and sells hydrofluoric acid in China
Blue Express (Shanghai) International Trade Inc. (China) - procures and sells high-purity chemicals
Blue Express (Shanghai) International Freight Forwarding Co., Ltd. (China) - transportation and customs brokerage
Quzhou BDX New Chemical Materials Co., Ltd. (Quzhou, Zhejiang) - manufactures LiPF6 and high-purity chemical products
Blue Express, Inc. (Sakai, Osaka) - general logistics, procurement and sales of HF and chemicals
Stella Express (Singapore) Pte Ltd. - transportation and customs brokerage in Singapore
Stella Pharma Corporation (Osaka) - medical business, BNCT (Boron Neutron Capture Therapy) cancer treatment
Blue Auto Trust Co., Ltd. (Sakai, Osaka) - insurance agency and automotive repair
_externalRelationships.notes: Twelve-nine (99.9999999999%) purity HF - only Stella and Morita Chemical can achieve this globally. Founded 1916 as Morita Chemical's fluorine division spinoff precursor, Osaka-based. High-purity chemicals segment is 86% of consolidated sales. Semiconductor applications ~70% of revenue. Battery/energy segment growing fastest (~15% of revenue, doubled in FY2025). Company has been diversifying raw material procurement away from China to Europe and Mexico to reduce supply chain risk. Maruzen Chemicals and Mitsubishi Chemical are the two named major revenue customers in securities filings. The LiPF6 supply chain runs: Stella Chemifa (LiPF6) -> Mitsubishi Chemical (electrolyte formulation, Sol-Rite brand) -> Panasonic (battery cells) -> Tesla/EV OEMs. Also supplies Japan's sole manufacturer of BF3 (boron trifluoride) gas for organic synthesis catalysts. Enriched boron-10 technology used for nuclear fuel storage and BNCT cancer therapy (via subsidiary Stella Pharma). FY2025/3 Q3 results: revenue 26.96B JPY (+1.2% YoY), operating profit 3.58B JPY (+7.4% YoY). Dividend yield: 3.21%. ROE forecast: 6.01%. 52-week range: 3,130-5,940 JPY. Industry has fewer than 15 companies globally capable of producing EG-HF. Top 5 suppliers control ~70% of global market.

_metadata.dataSource: MoneyPost, Company IR, Stock Analysis, IRBank, MarketScreener, MarketsandMarkets, Bloomberg BNEF, IndustryWeek, Stella Chemifa official website, Kabutan, Wikipedia, Handotai-Gyokai, industry market reports
_metadata.notes: Comprehensive update: added 5 new fab clients (Kioxia, SMIC, Hua Hong, UMC, GlobalFoundries), identified 2 named major customers (Maruzen Chemicals distributor, Mitsubishi Chemical for LiPF6), mapped LiPF6 battery supply chain (Stella->Mitsubishi Chemical->Panasonic->Tesla), added 13 competitors, identified raw material supply chain (fluorite from China/Europe/Mexico), documented all 9 subsidiaries including China JVs and Singapore manufacturing. Updated financial data to Feb 2026: market cap 68.63B JPY, PER 21.62x, PBR 1.39x, EPS 244.83 JPY, price 5,290 JPY, foreign ownership 25.93%.
